hyperedges.summary: The five prior notes plus EGGROLL close into a single architectural schema: PPCA. Closure hyperedge encodes that formulation v1, analog primitive gaps, device-math mapping, and the ES Stage-2 branch jointly produce the EGGROLL-PPCA architecture.

One outer EGGROLL-PPCA step cycles through six phases: perturbation draw → combined forward → nonlinearity → inter-layer analog routing → fitness scoring → Gilbert-cell consolidation. Closes back to phase 1 after τ_slow consolidation.

nodes.source_location: ## Triangular Structure of the Three Primitives
nodes.author: ws
nodes.norm_label: triangular structure: log-domain (algebra), gilbert (topology), subthreshold exp (device regime) are three views of one fact

nodes.label: Stage-2 Architecture — EGGROLL-PPCA (Perturbation-Pair Crossbar Array)
nodes.summary: Closes Stage-2 learning-rule question from formulation v1 by committing to score-function/ES (EGGROLL) realized as a dual-crossbar (slow G + fast rank-1 ab^T) analog architecture with Gilbert-cell consolidation and subthreshold fitness. Forward-only, all-analog critical path; natural batch size K_batch = τ_slow/τ_fast falls out of device physics. Structural hub unifying five communities.

nodes.label: Phase 1 — Perturbation Draw (counter-RNG a,b ~ N(0,σ²))
nodes.summary: Counter-based deterministic RNG produces reproducible a ∈ ℝ^m and b ∈ ℝ^n; written as analog voltages onto the perturbation crossbar rows/columns. Regeneratable at consolidation without storage.

nodes.label: Phase 2 — Combined Forward Pass (y = Gx + α·a·(b·x))
nodes.summary: Input x broadcast to both crossbars. Base crossbar emits Gx via Kirchhoff; perturbation crossbar emits a·(b·x) as a rank-1 structured product. Output summed at current node. One crossbar latency + one rank-1 broadcast — not two MVMs.
nodes.norm_label: phase 2 — combined forward pass (y = gx + α·a·(b·x))

nodes.label: Phase 3 — Nonlinearity N_α via Kink-Biased MOSFETs
nodes.summary: Column of kink-biased MOSFETs implements piecewise-linear N_kink activation (formulation v1 Option α). Activation function is fixed by kink/punch-through I–V device physics, not software.
nodes.norm_label: phase 3 — nonlinearity n_α via kink-biased mosfets

nodes.label: Phase 4 — Inter-Layer Analog Routing (no intermediate digitization)
nodes.summary: Intermediate layer outputs z stay analog and feed the next PPCA layer's x directly. D comparator is only invoked at final output — minimizes ADC overhead. Relies on shared-silicon claim: final-layer MOSFETs dual-role as N+D, earlier layers only N.
nodes.norm_label: phase 4 — inter-layer analog routing (no intermediate digitization)

nodes.label: Phase 5 — Fitness Scoring S = exp(−||z−t||²/2σ²) (single subthreshold transistor)
nodes.summary: Gaussian-approximate EGGROLL score realized in a single subthreshold-exponential transistor; outputs scalar S as a current level on a reference wire.
nodes.norm_label: phase 5 — fitness scoring s = exp(−||z−t||2/2σ2) (single subthreshold transistor)

nodes.label: Phase 6 — Consolidation ΔG_ij = η·S·a_i·b_j via Gilbert-Cell Strip
nodes.summary: Strip of Gilbert cells evaluates four-quadrant S·a_i·b_j; currents program charge-trap cells of the base crossbar. Slow (μs) programming acts as analog low-pass filter over many fast Phase 1–5 iterations; no digital accumulator needed.
nodes.norm_label: phase 6 — consolidation δg_ij = η·s·a_i·b_j via gilbert-cell strip

nodes.label: Lever 1 — Tiling (N×N → (N/k)² tiles)
nodes.summary: Logical N×N crossbar implemented as (N/k)² physical tiles (k≈256). Independent during Phase 2 (no horizontal dependency in Kirchhoff summation). Tile pipelining. Latency O(log(N/k)), area O(N²).

nodes.label: Lever 2 — Rank-r Perturbation with Precision-for-Rank Tradeoff
nodes.summary: EGGROLL perturbation is rank-r ≪ min(m,n). Write bandwidth O(r(m+n)) not O(mn). Pair-3 identity r ≤ b·redundancy links cell bit-depth to usable rank: a hardware design knob, not an algorithmic one.
nodes.norm_label: lever 2 — rank-r perturbation with precision-for-rank tradeoff

nodes.label: Lever 3 — Two-Timescale Pipelining (τ_slow ≈ 10 μs, τ_fast ≈ 100 ns)
nodes.summary: Charge-trap programming τ_slow ≈ 10 μs; floating-bulk perturbation and forward/fitness ≈ 100 ns. Natural pipelining of fast inner loop against slow consolidation.
nodes.norm_label: lever 3 — two-timescale pipelining (τ_slow ≈ 10 μs, τ_fast ≈ 100 ns)

nodes.label: Timescale Identity K_batch = τ_slow / (τ_fast_perturb + τ_forward + τ_fitness) ≈ 50
nodes.summary: The natural number of perturbations per consolidation is derived from device timescales, not chosen. ≈ 10 μs / 200 ns ≈ 50, which matches typical EGGROLL K. Falls out of physics; ties device property to algorithmic hyperparameter.
nodes.norm_label: timescale identity k_batch = τ_slow / (τ_fast_perturb + τ_forward + τ_fitness) ≈ 50

nodes.label: Stage-2 Commitment to Score-Function / ES (EGGROLL)
nodes.summary: Of formulation v1's three Stage-2 options, ES is chosen because its five required operations (perturbation draw, forward pass, fitness, outer-product update, consolidation) all already have device-native realizations in the graph. The other two options add teacher wiring or a backward path.

nodes.label: Rejection — Layer-Local (NoProp / Hebbian + Global Signal)
nodes.summary: Layer-local Stage-2 option rejected: requires per-layer teacher wiring, breaks forward-only purity, adds per-layer area cost. Documented as explicit graph-level incompatibility so future additions do not silently reopen Stage-2.

nodes.label: Rejection — Surrogate / Straight-Through Estimator
nodes.summary: Surrogate-gradient Stage-2 option rejected: requires an explicit backward path and ~2× silicon area; violates forward-only all-analog critical-path requirement.

nodes.label: PPCA Structural Hub — Five-Community God-Node Candidate
nodes.summary: The architecture's neighbors span five distinct communities: Analog Multiplication Family (W, Gilbert), Subthreshold Nonlinearity (N, fitness), Two-Timescale Bridge (charge-trap, floating-bulk), Bit-Level Physics (encoder), Local/Non-Backprop Learning (ES). High betweenness by construction.
nodes.norm_label: ppca structural hub — five-community god-node candidate

nodes.label: Dual-Crossbar Topology — Base G (charge-trap) + Perturbation a·b^T (floating-bulk)
nodes.summary: Two co-located crossbars: the slow parameter G uses charge-trap long-term retention; the fast rank-1 perturbation α·ab^T uses floating-bulk short-term plasticity. Gilbert-cell strip at bottom consolidates.
nodes.norm_label: dual-crossbar topology — base g (charge-trap) + perturbation a·b^t (floating-bulk)

nodes.label: Shared-Silicon Dual Role — Final-Layer MOSFETs Serve N and D
nodes.summary: The D comparator array reuses the same MOSFET species at different bias. Only the final layer's MOSFETs serve dual-role as N+D; earlier layers' MOSFETs serve only as N. Instantiates formulation v1's shared_silicon_claim.
nodes.norm_label: shared-silicon dual role — final-layer mosfets serve n and d

nodes.label: Forward-Only All-Analog Critical Path
nodes.summary: No backward pass, no digitization between layers, no digital accumulator for consolidation. The entire critical path from x to ΔG is analog; only the final D comparator and optional output read are digital.

nodes.label: Analog Low-Pass Consolidation on Charge-Trap
nodes.summary: G(t+T_slow) = G(t) + η·Σ_k S_k · a_k · b_k^T. The floating-gate trap integrates successive subthreshold programming currents; no explicit digital accumulator. Charge-trap slowness IS the low-pass filter.

nodes.label: Stage-3 Hints — Open Problems Leaving PPCA
nodes.summary: Three open problems: (1) Gaussian-score learnability under charge-trap noise at O(r⁻¹); (2) cross-tile perturbation correlation biasing the gradient estimator; (3) multi-task/continual learning hierarchy from the two-timescale math.
nodes.norm_label: stage-3 hints — open problems leaving ppca

nodes.label: Rank-1 Perturbation Silicon Realization
nodes.summary: Phase 2's a·(b·x) as one column-sum inner product broadcast across a is the silicon realization of EGGROLL's rank-1 perturbation trick — one crossbar latency plus a broadcast, not two MVMs.

nodes.label: Natural Operating Point at Theoretical O(r⁻¹) Rate
nodes.summary: If EGGROLL's O(r⁻¹) rank-convergence theorem holds, the architecture's physics-derived K_batch ≈ 50 already places it at the theoretical rate — no tuning required.
nodes.norm_label: natural operating point at theoretical o(r−1) rate

nodes.label: Device-Primitive Completeness — Every Op Has a Graph Node
nodes.summary: Every row of the Phase→Operation→Primitive→Graph-Node table has a graph-resident primitive. Nothing in the architecture is invented without prior device grounding.
nodes.norm_label: device-primitive completeness — every op has a graph node

hyperedges.summary: The five prior notes plus EGGROLL close into a single architectural schema: PPCA. Closure hyperedge encodes that formulation v1, analog primitive gaps, device-math mapping, and the ES Stage-2 branch jointly produce the EGGROLL-PPCA architecture.

One outer EGGROLL-PPCA step cycles through six phases: perturbation draw → combined forward → nonlinearity → inter-layer analog routing → fitness scoring → Gilbert-cell consolidation. Closes back to phase 1 after τ_slow consolidation.
